message: fix: keep json output for empty search results

files.patch:
@@ -51,6 +51,7 @@
 {"id":"xf-27","title":"Improve empty results message with suggestions","description":"## Task

When search returns no results, provide helpful suggestions instead of a plain 'No results found.'

## Current State

'No results found.'
Not helpful - user doesn't know what to try next.

## Target State

No results for "xyzzy"

  Try:
    • Using different keywords
    • Checking your spelling
    • Removing date filters
    • Searching a different data type: xf search "xyzzy" --types dm

## Implementation

1. Detect empty results in cmd_search()
2. Analyze the query to provide context-aware suggestions:
   - If using filters, suggest removing them
   - If query is complex, suggest simplifying
   - If using --types, suggest trying other types

## Files to Modify

- src/main.rs: cmd_search() empty result handling
- src/repl.rs: run_search() empty result handling

## Acceptance Criteria

- Empty results show helpful suggestions
- Suggestions are context-aware when possible","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:51:29.269041-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:09:13.104945-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:09:13.104945-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: hide scores, add timing, improve empty results message","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-27","depends_on_id":"xf-14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.502668783-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"xf-28","title":"Remove unsearchable types from --types values","description":"## Task

Remove non-searchable types (follower, following, block, mute) from the --types possible values in search command.

## Current State

--types shows: tweet, like, dm, grok, follower, following, block, mute, all

But follower, following, block, mute have no text content to search. Including them confuses users and returns empty results.

## Target State

--types shows: tweet, like, dm, grok

The 'all' option should either be removed or documented to only search types with content.

## Implementation

1. In src/cli.rs, modify the SearchableType or DataType enum
2. Either create a separate SearchableType enum or filter DataType in search context
3. Update the possible_values for --types arg

## Files to Modify

- src/cli.rs: Modify possible_values or create SearchableType

## Consideration

Decide what to do with 'all':
- Option A: Remove 'all' entirely
- Option B: Keep 'all' but document it only searches content types
- Option C: Create 'content' alias that means tweet+like+dm+grok

## Acceptance Criteria

- xf search --help shows only searchable types
- Using invalid types gives clear error","notes":"Completed via SearchType in xf-16; search --help now lists tweet/like/dm/grok/all only with examples and tests; invalid types now rejected by clap. See help_text_test.sh + cli_e2e help test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:51:41.337913-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:52:05.727529499-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T12:51:49.884456408-05:00","close_reason":"Completed as part of xf-16: search --types now limited to searchable types with help/test coverage","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-28","depends_on_id":"xf-16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.50541071-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"xf-29","title":"Enhance REPL startup banner with archive info","description":"## Task

Replace the plain startup message with a rich banner showing archive info and version.

## Current State

'xf interactive mode. Type 'help' for commands, 'quit' to exit.'

Plain, no context about what archive is loaded.

## Target State

╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  xf shell v0.5.0                                             │
│                                                              │
│  Archive: @username                                          │
│  Tweets: 12,345 • DMs: 2,341 • Likes: 8,234                  │
│                                                              │
│  Type 'help' for commands, 'quit' to exit                    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

## Implementation

1. Load archive stats from storage at REPL startup
2. Get version from env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
3. Format the banner with box-drawing characters
4. Print before entering the REPL loop

## Files to Modify

- src/repl.rs: run() function, add banner printing
- src/storage.rs: May need method to get quick stats

## Acceptance Criteria

- Banner shows version, username, content counts
- Box drawing characters align correctly
- Graceful fallback if stats unavailable","notes":"Ensure banner adapts to terminal width and missing stats; fall back to minimal line when stats unavailable. Test plan: unit tests for banner width calculations and formatting; e2e script that runs REPL startup with a fixture archive and logs command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing, and env.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:51:53.294341-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:54:26.284150967-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:32.718603-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented as part of xf-17. Startup banner now shows archive username, document counts, and version.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-29","depends_on_id":"xf-17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.507569438-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"xf-2dh","title":"Ensure JSON search output remains JSON when no results","description":"cmd_search prints human 'No results found' text even for --format json/json-pretty, breaking machine-parseable output. Emit [] for JSON outputs when result set is empty; keep human guidance for text/compact/csv.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:17:39.008558895-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:19:06.601392926-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:19:06.601392926-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: emit JSON empty array when search has no results"}
 {"id":"xf-30","title":"Add did-you-mean suggestions for typos","description":"## Task

When users mistype command names or type values, suggest the correct option using fuzzy matching.

## Current State

'Unknown command: serach' or 'Unknown type: twet'
No suggestion for what they probably meant.

## Target State

Unknown command: 'serach'

  Did you mean 'search'?
  
  Type 'help' for available commands.

## Implementation

1. Implement Levenshtein distance function or use strsim crate
2. When parsing fails, find closest match within edit distance of 2
3. Suggest the match if confidence is high enough

fn suggest_similar(input: &str, options: &[&str]) -> Option<&str> {
    options.iter()
        .filter_map(|opt| {
            let dist = strsim::levenshtein(input, opt);
            if dist <= 2 { Some((opt, dist)) } else { None }
        })
        .min_by_key(|(_, dist)| *dist)
        .map(|(opt, _)| *opt)
}

## Files to Modify

- Cargo.toml: Add strsim = "0.10" if not present
- src/repl.rs: parse_command() error handling
- src/cli.rs or main.rs: Type parsing errors

## Acceptance Criteria

- Typos within 2 edits suggest correct spelling
- Only suggests if there's a clear match
- Works for commands, types, and targets","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:52:06.676356-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:55.792301-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:55.792301-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Levenshtein distance in error.rs and integrated did-you-mean suggestions for --fields and config keys. Used native implementation (no strsim dependency).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-30","depends_on_id":"xf-15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.509665458-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"xf-31","title":"Fix CSV export missing carriage return handling","description":"## Bug

The CSV export in repl.rs replaces \n with space but leaves \r intact, which can cause issues in some spreadsheet software.

## Current Code (repl.rs:887)

let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace('\n', " ");

## Fix

let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace('\n', " ").replace('\r', "");

Or better:
let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace(['\n', '\r'], " ");

## Files to Modify

- src/repl.rs: run_export() function
- src/main.rs: CSV output sections (if any)

## Testing

- Export data with Windows-style line endings (\r\n)
- Verify CSV opens correctly in Excel and other spreadsheet software

## Acceptance Criteria

- No \r characters in CSV output
- CSV is valid RFC 4180 format","notes":"CSV escaping now uses shared csv_escape_text across search output, export (serde-driven CSV), and REPL export; CR/LF sanitized and quotes escaped consistently. Unit tests cover CR/LF/quote handling; cli_e2e tests exercised with --nocapture.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:52:19.047685-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T11:33:46.822693307-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T11:33:46.822693307-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-31","depends_on_id":"xf-19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T02:36:53.511744526-05:00","created_by":"import"}]}
